title: crataegus acutifolia var. insignis
intro: Crataegus acutifolia var. insignis, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a deciduous tree or shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is prized for its medicinal properties, including its ability to dilate blood vessels and improve cardiovascular health, thanks to its high content of flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crataegus acutifolia var. insignis include reducing blood pressure, improving circulation, and protecting against heart failure, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves, flowers, and berries of the hawthorn tree are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
